channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: FG commiserates with China over tragic landslide in Gansu Province
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government has commiserated with the Government and people of China over the tragic landslide in Gansu Province. The post FG commiserates with China over tragic landslide in Gansu Province appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Labour vows legal action against FG, others over planned sale of Unity Schools
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: • Warns Presidency, Kings College alumni association The Association of Senior Civil Servants of Nigeria (ASCSN) yesterday threatened to institute legal action against the Presidency or any other body planning to concession or sell Federal Government Colleges to private interests. Charging President

item.title: WACT-APM Terminals Nigeria graduates first cohort of EngineerHer programme
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: WACT-APM Terminals Nigeria has marked the graduation of the first cohort of its EngineerHer programme, a one-year initiative designed to equip female graduates with practical skills, industry exposure and clear pathways into employment. Launched in 2025, the programme is designed to increase female

item.title: Truecaller clashes with India’s telecom regulator over anti-spam rules
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The caller ID company says users are increasingly ignoring and blocking calls from India's dedicated business number series.

item.title: Wanted bandit kingpin captured in Delta forest
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Anti-Kidnapping Squad of the Delta State Police Command alongside SafeCity Security Service has captured a wanted bandit, Abubakar Usman, in his hideout in the Ogwashi-Uku/Adonta Forest. The post Wanted bandit kingpin captured in Delta forest appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Copper Rises as Risk Assets Shrug Off More US Strikes on Iran
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Copper snapped a two-day loss as it shrugged off a fresh round of US strikes on Iran to focus on optimism over a longer-term demand boost from artificial intelligence.

item.title: ADC tightens control over party litigation, directs state chapters to refer court processes to secretariat
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has directed all its state chapters and officials, across the country, to refer all court processes served on the party to the National Legal Adviser. The post ADC tightens control over party litigation, directs state chapters to refer court processes to secret

item.title: No aggrieved Oyo APC aspirant’ll be sidelined -Reconciliation panel
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Ahead of the 2027 general elections, the newly inaugurated Reconciliation Committee of the All Progressives Congress (APC) in Oyo State has assured aggrieved members and aspirants that no one would be sidelined in the party’s ongoing peace process. The post No aggrieved Oyo APC aspirant’ll be sideli

item.title: NUJ honours Amuwo-Odofin LG chairman Lanre Sanusi as 2026 media games ambassador
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Nigeria Union of Journalists (NUJ), Lagos State Council, has honoured the Chairman of Amuwo-Odofin Local Government, Prince Lanre Sanusi, with the Media Games Ambassador award in recognition of his contributions to grassroots development and responsive governance. The post NUJ honours Amuwo-Odof

item.title: Northern govs, traditional rulers back state police, strengthening of regional security
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Governors of the 19 Northern states and members of the Northern Traditional Rulers’ Council have reaffirmed their support for the establishment of state police and pledged stronger regional collaboration to tackle the worsening security situation across the region. The post Northern govs, traditiona

item.title: Authors of our problems can’t be our saviours, NYCN slams northern security trust fund board
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Northern Youth Council of Nigeria (NYCN), Isah Abubakar, has criticised the composition of the newly constituted Board of Trustees of the Northern Nigeria Security Trust Fund (NNSTF). The post Authors of our problems can’t be our saviours, NYCN slams northern security trust fund board appeared f

item.title: Senate passes Customs’ N1.295trn 2026 budget
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Senate has approved the Nigerian Customs Service (NCS) 2026 budget with a proposed revenue target of N11.074 trillion and an expenditure estimate of N1.295 trillion. The post Senate passes Customs’ N1.295trn 2026 budget appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Lagos rescues unconscious pregnant woman, hands mother, baby to Plateau govt
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Chioma Obinna A 22-year-old pregnant woman found unconscious on a Lagos street has been rescued from the brink of death, delivered of a healthy baby girl and reunited with the Plateau State Government in what officials described as another success story of the Lagos State emergency healthcare int

item.title: Senate moves to impose 15-year jail term, asset forfeiture on fake drug dealers
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Manufacturers and distributors of fake drugs, counterfeit medical products and unwholesome processed foods could face up to 15 years imprisonment, multi-million-naira fines, forfeiture of assets and mandatory compensation for victims if a bill before the Senate eventually becomes law. The post Senat

item.title: Nigeria, Ghana strengthen maritime ties with MoU
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Nigerian Maritime Administration and Safety Agency, NIMASA, and the Ghana Maritime Authority (GMA) have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to strengthen bilateral maritime cooperation, enhance regional maritime governance, and promote the sustainable development of the Blue Economy acros

item.title: Adani Pressed by Judge for More Details on DOJ Move to Drop Case
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A US judge pressed Gautam Adani for more details on the Justice Department’s move to dismiss a blockbuster criminal case against the Indian billionaire.

item.title: US Senate nominee Platner halts campaign after assault allegations
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: A string of high-profile Democrats have withdrawn support for Platner following sexual assault allegation.

item.title: LASWA, Interferry advance seamanship training under ferry safety development programme
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Lagos State Waterways Authority (LASWA), in partnership with Interferry, has delivered Module Two (Seamanship) of the Ferry Safety Development Programme (FSDP), marking another milestone in Africa’s first structured ferry professional development initiative. The module focused on Ropes and Knots

item.title: Messi, Mbappé Lead Cast as Q’finals Begin Live on SuperSport
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: The race for FIFA World Cup 2026 glory enters its decisive stage this week as eight nations battle for four semi-final places, with football’s biggest stars and one of the

item.title: Wimbledon: Nigeria’s Ogunsakin Reaches Junior Doubles Q’finals with Dominant Win
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Nigeria’s tennis  Sensation, Oluwaseun Peter Ogunsakin, 17, and his Botswana partner, Ntungamili Raguin, have qualified for the Boys’ Doubles Quarter-finals at the 2026 Wimbledon Junior Championships. The African pair defeated the

item.title: S’Africa Names Squad for C’wealth Fencing Championships in Lagos
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: South Africa has announced a formidable 36-member squad for the 2026 Commonwealth Fencing Federation Championships in Lagos, underscoring its reputation as one of Africa’s fencing powerhouses. At the heart of

item.title: Portugal Confirms Departure of Coach Martínez
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: The Portuguese Football Federation (FPF) has confirmed the departure of national team coach Roberto Martinez following Portugal’s last-16 loss to Spain. Martinez had already announced that he would be leaving the

item.title: Africa’s Last Team Standing, Morocco, in Make or Mar Clash with Favourites France

item.description: Duro Ikhazuagbe  The first quarterfinal fixture of the 2026 FIFA World Cup between France and Morocco in Boston tonight presents a perfect chance for the Atlas Lions to get their

item.title: Confirmed Ebola deaths in DR Congo hit 600
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The number of deaths comes as healthcare workers threaten to walk off the job over a delay in payments.

item.title: Japan Donates Badminton Equipment to Boost Grassroots Devt in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: The Badminton Federation of Nigeria (BFN) has received a major boost to its grassroots and elite development programmes following the donation of badminton equipment by the Badminton Association of Japan

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Uzoma Mba  One hundred high school students drawn from 20 schools located in Nnewi South Local Government Area of Anambra state were in May 2026 trained on how to administer

item.title: Beyond the Fare: How Nigerian Drivers are Restoring Faith in Strangers
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: While headlines often focus on crime and dishonesty, countless acts of goodwill go unnoticed. Across Nigeria, ride-hailing drivers are quietly rewriting that narrative, showing that the greatest journeys are sometimes

item.title: At Chatham House, Kalu Addresses Nigeria’s Electoral Process Amid Security Crisis
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Emeka Emole “At a time when many seek to define Nigeria by its challenges, you continue to work with remarkable resilience and consistency to redefine our national image through knowledge-driven

item.title: United Nigeria Airlines offers hotel discounts for loyalty members
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Domestic carrier, United Nigeria Airlines, has entered into a strategic partnership with Boulevard Hotels to offer members of its Unity Rewards loyalty programme exclusive hotel benefits. The incentives offered in the programme include: a 25 percent discount on room rates, complimentary breakfast, e

item.title: THE FUTURE OF NORTHERN NIGERIA
item.dc:creator: ThisDay Editorial
item.description: The North must confront difficult truths that have too often been avoided or obscured by political correctness The 2nd Liberty Symposium recently held in Abuja with a focus on the

item.title: Invest in postharvest engineering to curb food losses —Don
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A professor of Postharvest Engineering, Energy Studies and Environmental Management at the University of Ibadan, Ademola Kabir Aremu, has advocated increased investment in postharvest engineering He said it is a cost-effective strategy for reducing food losses, improving food availability and streng

item.title: Chief Future Officer: Kate Gulliver, Wayfair
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: This episode profiles Wayfair CFO Kate Gulliver, who is leading the home goods retailer's efforts to achieve profitable growth during a challenging period for the housing market -- as well as launching a new initiative to open brick-and-mortar flagship stores. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: NAHCO appoints two new directors into board
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Nigerian Aviation Handling Company PLC (nahco Aviance) has announced the appointment of two new directors to its board. The newly appointed non-executive directors are Mr. Wolemi Esan, SAN and Dr. Julius B. Omodayo-Owotuga. The appointments came on the heels of the retirement of two long-serving

item.title: How Japan's Top Eyewear Maker is Passing Inflation Test
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: JINS rose to prominence 25 years ago with its bargain-basement ¥4,990 eyeglasses, becoming a household name for cheap chic as falling or stagnant prices took hold. Now, the Tokyo-based lensmaker is positioning itself to take advantage of the shift toward greater pricing power by opening up stores in

item.title: US Launches Strikes on Iran for Second Straight Day
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The US military said it struck Iran for the second straight day, an escalation of violence that threatens to strain an already fragile ceasefire. Bloomberg's Jeff Mason has the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: NAFDAC DG Adeyeye Honoured At UK House Of Lords For Regulatory Reforms
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Director General of Nigeria's National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC), Professor Mojisola Christianah Adeyeye, has received the Special African Leadership Commendation Award at the 16th African Business Leadership Awards (ABLA) held at the House of Lords in London.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: …Marwa seeks national action plan to tackle substance abuse By Kingsley Omonobi The Federal Government has reaffirmed its commitment to combating illicit drug use by dismantling drug trafficking networks, expanding access to treatment and rehabilitation, and promoting the wellbeing of Nigerians. Sec

item.title: BOK’s Shin Sees Need for Rate Hike Ahead of Next Week’s Meeting
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bank of Korea Governor Shin Hyun Song told parliament that policy rates will need to rise at an appropriate time, reinforcing the bank’s hawkish stance just a week before a widely expected rate hike.

item.title: NCAA backs initiatives to advance cabin crew safety
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Nigeria Civil Aviation Authority (NCAA) has reaffirmed its commitment to supporting cabin crew safety initiatives and ensuring passengers continue to get best travel experience through stronger collaboration with relevant industry stakeholders. Director-General of Civil Aviation (DGCA), Capt. Ch

item.title: Trump to ask US Supreme Court for new hearing on birthright citizenship
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: In June, the Supreme Court ruled against Trump’s order to end birthright citizenship for individuals born in the US.

item.title: Rights group plans complaint to IOC against Infantino over Trump support
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: FairSquare says it will lodge the complaint regarding the FIFA president's 'repeated breach of political neutrality'.

item.title: Morocco dealt huge blow as injured Saibari out of World Cup quarterfinal
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Ismael Saibari will miss the match against France in Boston due to a hamstring injury sustained last week.

item.title: Eight killed as landslide hits Rohingya school in Bangladesh
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: At least seven children and a teacher were killed after a landslide hit a girls’ school at a refugee camp in Bangladesh.

item.title: NMA secures bail for Prof. Martin Aghaji after DSS arrest over alleged economic crime
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Chioma Obinna The Nigerian Medical Association (NMA) has secured the release on bail of renowned consultant cardiac surgeon, Prof. (Dr.) Martin Aghaji, after he was arrested and detained by operatives of the Department of State Services (DSS) over an alleged economic crime. Aghaji was arrested at

item.title: US launches new strikes on Iran, Trump says they could get 'much worse'
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The US military launched a new round of strikes against Iran on Wednesday, following President Donald Trump's assertion that the fragile ceasefire between the two countries was over. Trump warned that the attacks could get "much worse" if Iran perpetrated further attacks on ships in the Strait of Ho

item.title: SAATM drives African airlines’ race for regional market share
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Africa’s airline industry is entering a new phase of competition as carriers intensify efforts to capture a larger share of the continent’s regional air travel market through fleet expansion, network growth and strategic partnerships. Driven by the implementation of the Single African Air Transport

item.description: Jeans maker raises its full-year outlook for the second straight time, but shares fall after hours

item.title: South Sudan, 15 years on: Still fighting for peace
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: A stalled peace process, deepening humanitarian needs and economic challenges test the world's youngest nation.

item.title: NZ Manufacturing Hits 5-Year High as Breman Highlights Recovery
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: New Zealand manufacturing expanded at the fastest pace in almost five years in June as central bank Governor Anna Breman pointed to a burgeoning economic recovery, underscoring this week’s decision to raise interest rates.

item.title: Bristow inaugurates first Airbus H160 helicopter for offshore operations
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Bristow Group has formally introduced an Airbus H160 helicopter into service in Nigeria, marking the aircraft’s first deployment in support of the energy sector and rescue services within the Group and on the African continent. The unveiling, held recently before regulators, energy stakeholders, and

item.title: Tehran releases footage showing ruins of late Iranian leader’s compound
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Iranian state media has released what it calls the first footage showing damage to the late Khamenei’s compound.

item.title: Supreme Court goes fully digital, ditches paper records to curb tampering
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ikechukwu Nnochiri On April 23, Vanguard Law & Human Rights highlighted some of the challenges plaguing the Nigerian judiciary, chief among them the judiciary’s continued reliance on analogue systems. This factor, it noted, was partly responsible for the slow pace of justice administration in

item.title: Before banning textile imports
item.dc:creator: The Sun Editorial
item.description: The Senate has called on the federal government to ban the importation of textile materials and map out plans to resuscitate the local textile industry and create more jobs. The call is coming on the heels of recent revelation that over $6 billion is spent annually on imported fabrics. The Red Chamb

item.title: Khamenei’s coffin in Karbala on last stop before burial in Mashhad
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Millions gathered in the holy city of Karbala, Iraq to pay their respects at the funeral of Iran’s late supreme leader.

item.title: Police CP engages Zamfara community on security issues
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: As part of ongoing efforts to enhance security and deepen community policing across Zamfara State, the Commissioner of Police, Zamfara State Command, CP A. M Bello, paid an operational visit to Bela and Gada communities in Bungudu Local Government Area, where he engaged traditional rulers, community

item.title: Zamfara gov’t digitalises public healthcare system
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Zamfara State Government launched a digital health initiative to improve healthcare delivery across hospitals and health facilities in the state. The Commissioner for Health, Dr Nafisa Muhammad Maradun, announced the initiative during the One-Day Digital Health Stakeholders’ Engagement Mee

item.title: Higher gas prices aren’t the only way rising tensions with Iran will hit home
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Wall Street expects President Donald Trump’s call that the Iran cease-fire is over will hurt airlines and home builders more than it will help oil companies.

item.title: Zamfara boosts emergency response, launches firefighting trucks
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Zamfara State government yesterday boosted its emergency response mechanisms by distributing eight firefighting trucks to some local government areas across the state. Commissioner for Works and Infrastructure, Lawal Barau Bungudu, represented by the Permanent Secretary of the ministry, Engr. Am

item.title: High Yields Set Stage for More Global Bond Inflow Into Indonesia
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Global inflows into Indonesian sovereign bonds are poised to extend, with investors saying they see value in some of emerging Asia’s highest yields on bets that a bruising round of interest-rate hikes is nearly over.

item.title: Benue Commissioner Tasks Healthcare Workers On Professionalism, reaffirms Commitment To Quality In Sector
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Benue State Government has reiterated commitment to strengthening the healthcare delivery system and ensure quality medical services for all citizens in the State. The post Benue Commissioner Tasks Healthcare Workers On Professionalism, reaffirms Commitment To Quality In Sector appeared first on Tr

item.title: Iran war live: Sirens in Bahrain, Kuwait after deadly US attacks on Iran
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US attacks kill at least one person in the city of Iranshahr and cause widespread power outages in Chabahar.

item.title: When Generals fall, Nigeria must confront security collapse
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE kidnapping and death of retired Major General Rabe Abubakar has become more than another tragic headline from Northern Nigeria. It is now a frightening national symbol of a country where insecurity has grown so widespread that even those once entrusted with defending the nation are no longer saf

item.title: Is Tinubu Nigeria’s saviour? Some genuinely think so! By Olu Fasan
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Recently, two weeks ago to be precise, I found myself in an ambush, facing something akin to a Spanish Inquisition. I had attended the wedding of a friend’s daughter in London. There I met some old Nigerian friends, some based in Nigeria, who I hadn’t seen for several years. We exchanged pleasantrie

item.title: Nigerian healthcare: Questions worth asking
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By LADE BANDELE How should a nation judge the performance of its health sector?” Public life has an odd way of allowing the loudest questions to eclipse the most important ones. A claim is made, a rebuttal follows, familiar positions harden, and attention gradually drifts from the matter under disc

item.title: NYSC reforms: Wrong step in the right direction
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By ANTHONY KILA Some institutions deserve our gratitude, even when they demand our courage to change them. The National Youth Service Corps is one of them. When the NYSC was established in 1973, Nigeria was emerging from the trauma of the civil war. The country needed more than roads and bridges; i

item.description: ELSEWHERE, I had posited that “every age, every generation, produces an iconoclast, an eclectic, eccentric, a quirky and unusual figure, who ironically represents the measure, the values, and indeed, the essence of that age or generation and becomes the icon of that age or generation. They are

item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: Burna Boy is heading to the first-ever FIFA World Cup Final Halftime Show. The Nigerian superstar will share the MetLife Stadium stage on 19 July 2026 with global icons Shakira, Madonna, BTS, and Justin Bieber. The post Burna Boy Joins Shakira, Madonna & Justin Bieber for FIFA’s Historic

item.title: Windstorm Leaves Seven Dead, Thousands Homeless in Jigawa
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: A powerful windstorm and heavy rainfall have left a trail of destruction across Jigawa State, killing at least seven people, injuring more than 1,400 others, and affecting over 5,400 residents in one of the state's worst weather disasters this year. The post Windstorm Leaves Seven Dead, Thousands Ho

item.title: Despite ‘misgivings,’ judge approves Elon Musk’s $1.5M SEC settlement
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The saga of Musk's tussle with the SEC over how he disclosed his growing stake in Twitter (now X) has come to an end.

item.title: Trump moves to remove Syria from US list of state sponsors of terrorism
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US President Donald Trump formally began the process of removing Syria from the US list of state sponsors of terrorism on Wednesday, notifying Congress after telling Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa the move would help rebuild the war-ravaged country. The decision marks a major shift in Washington's

item.title: Lagos Hosts Global Music Industry Leaders As AMBR, Music Tech Nigeria Return
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Lagos is once again set to take centre stage as Africa’s music industry capital when it hosts the second edition of the Africa Music Business Roundtable (AMBR) and Music Tech Nigeria, two of the continent’s fastest-growing conferences dedicated to music business, innovation and technolog

item.title: Olise faces suspension risk after FIFA rejects France appeal
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: World football governing body FIFA has turned down France’s appeal to overturn Michael Olise’s yellow card ahead of Thursday’s FIFA read more Olise faces suspension risk after FIFA rejects France appeal

item.title: Apple Supplier Luxshare Drops After $3 Billion Hong Kong Debut
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Apple Inc. supplier Luxshare Precision Industry Co. fell in Hong Kong trading debut on Thursday after raising HK$24.3 billion ($3.1 billion) in the city’s biggest listing this year so far.

item.title: Drug Party: NDLEA Appeals Pretty Mike’s Acquittal
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has urged the Lagos Division of the Court of Appeal to nullify the acquittal of Lagos socialite and nightclub owner, Mike Nwalie, popularly known as Pretty Mike, over alleged drug-related offences. Justice Ambrose Lewis-Allagoa had discharged Nwalie,

Tolulope Akande-Sadipe, has said tackling human trafficking requires both legislation and collaboration among government institutions, the private sector and

item.title: I started retirement with $3 million. Now I’m 89 and down to $2 million. What if I get sick?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Even if you’re not worried about your normal spending anymore, a long-term-care need is still a problem for any budget.

item.title: Fake Drugs: Senate Backs 15-Year Jail Term, Asset Forfeiture For Offenders
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday advanced moves to strengthen Nigeria’s fight against counterfeit medicines and unsafe food products, approving for second reading a bill proposing tougher penalties, asset forfeiture and stronger enforcement measures against offenders. The proposed Counterfeit Medical Product

item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The $300 million round is expected to be led by Menlo Ventures, Sifted reported.

item.title: Tinubu Rejects Raw Materials Council, Chartered Institute Of Purchasing Bills
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has declined assent to the Chartered Institute of Purchasing and Supply Management of Nigeria (Establishment) Amendment Bill, 2026, and the Raw Materials Research and Development Council (Amendment) Bill, 2026, citing legal, structural and drafting defects. The president’s deci

item.title: Meta is reportedly working on smart glasses that would be recording all the time
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Meta might be the next company to make an always-on AI wearable. The company is working on prototype "super sensing" always-aware smart glasses that could continuously record audio and snap photos "every few seconds," according to the Financial Times. The wearer could then ask Meta AI about the capt

item.title: Justin Bieber joins FIFA World Cup 2026 final halftime show
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Through its Unite for Education campaign, FIFA is also using the World Cup to raise awareness about education projects. Players wore special Unite for Education sleeve patches during

item.title: Chris Sutton's World Cup quarter-final predictions
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the quarter-final ties at the 2026 World Cup, including England against Norway.

item.title: Disability Forum Urges Tinubu To End Arrest Of PWDs
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Society for People with Disability Forum has called on President Bola Tinubu to direct security agencies and other government authorities to immediately end the arrest, detention and alleged extortion of persons with disabilities (PWDs) across the country. The appeal was contained in an open let

item.title: El-Rufai denies breaching court order, demands ICPC retract statement over hospital visit
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Mr El-Rufai's defence team has demanded the retraction of the ICPC's 7July publication titled “El Rufai and Medical Doctor Abuse Privilege, Violate Court Order.” The post El-Rufai denies breaching court order, demands ICPC retract statement over hospital visit appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria

item.title: 2027: City Boy Movement Will Deliver ‘Maximum Victory’ For Tinubu – Akpabio
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President of the Senate, Godswill Akpabio, has said the City Boy Movement is poised to ensure “maximum victory” for the re-election of President Bola Tinubu in 2027. Akpabio stated this on Wednesday while receiving a delegation of the group led by its Director-General, Francis Shoga, on

item.title: Reps Urge FG To End Ransom Cash Economy
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The House of Representatives had urged President Bola Tinubu to establish a coordinated inter–agency framework to disrupt ransom financing and strengthen financial intelligence collaboration among relevant security, regulatory, and financial institutions. It also urged the federal government to stre

item.title: Senate Rejects Motion To Probe PFIPC Scandal, Awaits ICPC Report
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …Senate has duty to probe N1.3bn PFIPC budget, Says Kawu The Senate, on Wednesday, rejected a motion seeking an immediate investigation into the controversy surrounding the Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC) for which N1.3 billion was appropriated in the 2026 budget. S

item.title: NUPRC Hands Over 19 Petroleum Prospecting Licences To Winners Of 2024 Licensing Round
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC) has formally executed concession contracts and commenced the issuance of Petroleum Prospecting Licences (PPLs) to successful bidders in the 2024 Licensing Round, marking a major step towards unlocking fresh investments in Nigeria’s

item.title: Academics, economists, others discuss African development at AFEA 2026 conference
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: “Debt is not bad in itself; the issue is when debt is borrowed but is not used for capital investment, the servicing of debt becomes a burden. The post Academics, economists, others discuss African development at AFEA 2026 conference appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.
